meta.title: SANAD Breakthrough-Seizure Outcomes
meta.authors: Bonnett LJ, Powell GA, Tudur Smith C, et al.
meta.outcome: Breakthrough seizure, subsequent seizure recurrence, and re-remission after a period of 12-month remission (SANAD)
meta.clinical_utility:
This tool shows what happens AFTER someone with newly-diagnosed epilepsy first becomes seizure-free for 12 months on medication (SANAD trial; Bonnett et al., PLOS ONE 2017). It is not a personal risk score — each curve is the average result for the whole group. Read the three curves as steps in a patient's journey:

STEP 1 — Will a breakthrough seizure happen? (Fig 2) From the moment of 12-month seizure-freedom, the risk of the FIRST 'breakthrough' seizure returning: ~24% by 1 year, rising to ~55% by 5–6 years. Many people stay seizure-free, but breakthroughs are common over time.

STEP 2a — If a breakthrough happens, will there be MORE seizures? (Fig 3) This curve applies ONLY once a breakthrough has already occurred — the clock RE-STARTS at that breakthrough seizure. It answers: having broken through, how likely is a further seizure? The answer is high — ~74% within 2 years (the next seizure came after a median of only ~1 month). So a single breakthrough usually means the epilepsy is active again; only about 1 in 4 stay seizure-free afterwards.

STEP 2b — If a breakthrough happens, can remission be regained? (Fig 4) The hopeful counterpart, describing the SAME people as Step 2a: the chance of re-achieving another full 12 months seizure-free. It is 0 before 12 months (by definition), then climbs to ~64% by 2 years — most people do eventually regain control.

Steps 2a and 2b are two views of the same patients who had a breakthrough: 2a = how soon the next seizure comes; 2b = how often long-term control returns. Use the outcome selector to switch curves and the time slider to read the probability at any month.

model.selector_info: SANAD-BT is three separate group-average curves, read as steps in a patient's journey after a first 12-month remission on treatment. Choose which to display: STEP 1 (Fig 2) risk of a FIRST breakthrough seizure; STEP 2a (Fig 3) — applies only AFTER a breakthrough — the risk of a FURTHER seizure (clock re-starts at the breakthrough); STEP 2b (Fig 4) — same people — the chance of regaining a 12-month remission. There is no patient-level score; each is an overall curve.

breakthrough.info: STEP 1 · Fig 2 — Risk of a FIRST breakthrough seizure. The clock starts when the patient first reaches 12 months seizure-free on treatment. ~24% at 1yr, ~37% at 2yr, ~55% by ~5.6yr. (34% of the whole cohort had a breakthrough.) The complement is the chance of STAYING seizure-free on treatment.

recurrence.display: Risk of seizure after a breakthrough (Fig 3)
recurrence.info: STEP 2a · Fig 3 — Applies ONLY once a breakthrough seizure has already happened; the clock RE-STARTS at that breakthrough. It answers: 'having broken through, how likely is a FURTHER seizure?' High — ~68% at 1yr, ~74% at 2yr, ~76% ceiling (the next seizure came after a median of only ~1 month). So only ~1 in 4 people stay seizure-free after a single breakthrough — a breakthrough usually signals the epilepsy is active again. (This is why the curve rises so steeply, unlike Fig 2.)

remission.info: STEP 2b · Fig 4 — the HOPEFUL counterpart to Step 2a, describing the SAME people (those who had a breakthrough): the chance of re-achieving ANOTHER 12-month remission. It is 0 before 12 months (a remission requires 12 seizure-free months by definition), then rises to ~29% one year past that point, ~64% at 2yr, ~82% ceiling. Most people eventually regain control. COSY is not shown — this is a favourable outcome, not a seizure-risk curve.

provenance.source_paper: PMID28698335_2017_Risk of a seizure recurrence after a breakthrough seizu.pdf
provenance.digitizer: riskcalc auto-trace (contiguous-run axis detection + topmost-dark trace)
provenance.notes: Values digitised from the paper's own Kaplan–Meier curves (Figs 2–4); the paper reports no score/points. Curve ceilings from the digitised traces: breakthrough-seizure risk rises to ~58% by ~5.6yr (24% at 1yr, 37% at 2yr); seizure-recurrence-after-breakthrough rises to a ~76% ceiling (68% at 1yr, 74% at 2yr); chance of re-achieving 12-month remission is flat until 12mo then rises to ~82% (29% at 1yr, 64% at 2yr). Separately, the paper's TEXT quotes flow-through fractions of the SANAD cohort (34% proceed to a first breakthrough, 63% of those go on to recurrence, 44% re-achieve remission) — these are cohort proportions, NOT the curve ceilings. Human review of the three overlays required before deploy.
